title: Schema descriptions carry no per-block rhetorical criteria; block writes are unvalidated against them
description: Each block schema's description states the block's purpose but omits the block's specific rhetorical demands/criteria for its entries (terseness, signal-density, self-containment, appropriateness to block type and downstream use, no narration or git/live-source restatement). The criteria must be embedded per-block and machine-checkable so a Claude Code hook can validate a block-item write against them at write time.

evidence.reference: the 'Rhetorical situation for every block write' rule lives only as operating-doc prose — not encoded per-schema, not enforced on write
item descriptions state field purpose but carry no per-block rhetorical criteria; nothing validates an entry against them on write

impact: Per-block authoring criteria are unenforced; off-contract entries (provenance narration, git restatement, non-terse prose) pass validation — degrading the verbatim context handed downstream to subagents (garbage in/out).
proposed_resolution: Resolution not yet determined; to be filed as a separate decision.
